tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows significantly elevated creatinine, proteinuria, microalbuminuria, and low urine specific gravity, suggesting impaired renal function, possibly chronic kidney disease. Urine is abnormal in appearance and color, with bilirubin and ketones present. LDL cholesterol is mildly elevated. HbA1c is low, indicating no evidence of chronic hyperglycemia. Recommend nephrology evaluation and further assessment for underlying renal pathology and possible hepatic involvement.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
Nonproliferative retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
